View this page

GedcomX API¶

Core GedcomX container types, collections, validation, and serialization entry points.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.gedcomx.TypeCollection(item_type)[source]¶

Bases: Generic[T]

A typed, indexable, iterable container with small indexes on id/name/uri. The class name stays ‘Collection’; the element type is carried in item_type.

pop(index=-1)[source]¶

Pop and return an item at the given index (default: last), updating all secondary indexes.

Return type:

TypeVar(T)

by_id(id_)[source]¶

Return the item with the given id, or None if not found.

Return type:

Optional[TypeVar(T)]

by_uri(uri)[source]¶

Return the item whose URI matches, or None if not found.

Return type:

Optional[TypeVar(T)]

by_name(sname)[source]¶

Return items whose name matches sname (stripped), or None if not found.

Return type:

list[TypeVar(T)] | None

append(item)[source]¶

Append an item to the collection and update all secondary indexes.

Return type:

None

Args:

item: The item to add; must be an instance of item_type.

Raises:

TypeError: If item is not an instance of item_type.

extend(items)[source]¶

Append each item from an iterable to the collection.

Return type:

None

remove(item)[source]¶

Remove an item from the collection and update all secondary indexes.

Return type:

None

Raises:

ValueError: If the item is not present in the collection.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.gedcomx.GedcomX(id=None, attribution=None, _filepath=None, description=None, persons=None, relationships=None, sourceDescriptions=None, agents=None, places=None, events=None, documents=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Main GedcomX Object representing a Genealogy. Stores collections of Top Level Gedcom-X Types. complies with GEDCOM X Conceptual Model V1 (http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1)

Parameters¶

idstr

Unique identifier for this Genealogy.

attributionAttribution Object

Attribution information for the Genealogy

filepathstr

Not Implemented.

descriptionstr

Description of the Genealogy: ex. ‘My Family Tree’

Raises¶

ValueError

If id is not a valid UUID.

version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
property contents¶

Return a dict with item counts for each top-level collection.

add(gedcomx_type_object)[source]¶

Dispatch a GedcomX top-level object to its appropriate add_* method.

Args:
gedcomx_type_object: A Document, Person, SourceDescription, Agent,

PlaceDescription, Event, or Relationship instance.

Raises:

ValueError: If the object type is not a recognised top-level type.

add_source_description(sourceDescription)[source]¶

Add a SourceDescription to the genealogy.

Args:

sourceDescription: The SourceDescription to add; must have an id.

Raises:

ValueError: If the argument is not a SourceDescription or has no id.

add_document(document)[source]¶

Add a Document object to the Genealogy

Args:

document: Document Object

Returns:

None

Raises:

ValueError: If document is not of type Document.

add_person(person)[source]¶

Add a Person object to the Genealogy

Args:

person: Person Object

Returns:

None

Raises:

ValueError: If person is not of type Person.

add_relationship(relationship)[source]¶

Add a Relationship to the genealogy.

Also registers any embedded Person objects and updates the internal relationship table so each person can quickly retrieve its relationships.

Args:

relationship: The Relationship to add.

Raises:

ValueError: If the argument is not a valid Relationship.

add_place_description(placeDescription)[source]¶

Add a PlaceDescription to the genealogy.

add_agent(agent)[source]¶

Add an Agent to the genealogy, skipping duplicates by id.

Args:

agent: The Agent to add.

Returns:

False if an agent with this id already exists (duplicate skipped); None (implicit return) if the agent was successfully added.

Raises:

ValueError: If the argument is not an Agent.

add_event(event_to_add)[source]¶

Add an Event to this GedcomX genealogy.

Automatically assigns a uid if the event has no id. Duplicate events (by equality) are silently ignored.

Args:

event_to_add: The Event object to add.

Raises:

ValueError: If event_to_add is None or not an Event instance.

extend(gedcomx)[source]¶

Merge all top-level objects from another GedcomX instance into this one.

get_person_by_id(obj_id)[source]¶

Return the Person with the given id, or None if not found.

source_by_id(obj_id)[source]¶

Return the SourceDescription with the given id, or None if not found.

validate()[source]¶

Validate this GedcomX document.

Recursively validates every object in every collection, then performs cross-collection checks (e.g. relationship person references resolve).

Return type:

ValidationResult

Returns:

ValidationResult with accumulated errors and warnings.

property id_index: Dict[Any, SourceDescription | Person | Relationship | Agent | Event | Document | PlaceDescription | Group]¶

Return a combined id→object mapping across all top-level collections.

classmethod from_dict(data)[source]¶

Deserialize a GedcomX instance from a JSON-compatible dict.

Return type:

GedcomX

gml()[source]¶

Return the GedcomX graph as a GML string.

Persons become nodes; Couple and ParentChild relationships become directed edges. See GedcomXGmlExporter for the full attribute list.

Return type:

str

Returns:

GML content as a str.

property json: bytes¶

Return the GedcomX document as indented UTF-8 JSON bytes.

Uses Serialization.serialize so resource references are emitted as {"resource": "#id"} pointers rather than inlined objects.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.person.Person(**data)[source]¶

Bases: Subject

A person in the GedcomX model.

identifier: ClassVar[str] = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Person'¶
version: ClassVar[str] = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'¶
private: bool | None¶
gender: Gender | None¶
names: List[Name]¶
facts: List[Fact]¶
living: bool | None¶
add_fact(fact_to_add)[source]¶

Add a Fact to this person, skipping duplicates. Returns True if the fact was added.

Return type:

bool

add_name(name_to_add)[source]¶

Add a Name to this person, skipping duplicates. Returns True if the name was added.

Return type:

bool

display()[source]¶

Return a display-summary dict with name, gender, lifespan, birth date, and death date.

Return type:

dict

property name: str | None¶

Return the full text of the primary name form, or None if unavailable.

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True, 'extra': 'allow', 'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(_Conclusion__context)¶

Populate derived state after model initialization.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.person.QuickPerson(name: str, dob: str | None = None, dod: str | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Convenience factory: QuickPerson("Jane Doe", dob="1900") returns a fully constructed Person.

list

FactType

static guess(description)[source]¶

Optional[FactType]

None

add_address(address_to_add)[source]¶

Add an Address to this agent, skipping duplicates.

Return type:

None

add_name(name_to_add)[source]¶

Add a name to this agent, skipping duplicates. Accepts a string or TextValue.

Return type:

None

add_identifier(identifier_to_add)[source]¶

Append an Identifier to this agent’s identifier list.

None

bool

gedcomtools.gedcomx.date.parse_to_gedcomx_date(s)[source]¶

Parse a natural-language or standard date string into a GedcomX formal date string.

Handles approximate prefixes (abt, circa), range expressions (between X and Y), and before/after qualifiers. Returns None if the input cannot be parsed.

Return type:

str

gedcomtools.gedcomx.date.date_to_timestamp(date_obj)[source]¶

Convert a Date object to a Python datetime by parsing its formal or original value.

Return type:

datetime | None

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.identifier.IdentifierList(identifiers=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

Maps identifier-type URI → list of URI values.

identifiers: Dict[str, List[URI]]¶
make_hashable(obj)[source]¶

Recursively convert obj to a hashable form for deduplication.

Return type:

Any

unique_list(items)[source]¶

Return a deduplicated list, preserving first-occurrence order.

Return type:

list

append(identifier)[source]¶

Append an Identifier to the list (alias for add_identifier).

Return type:

None

add_identifier(identifier)[source]¶

Merge the values of identifier into the corresponding type bucket, deduplicating.

Return type:

None

contains(identifier)[source]¶

Return True if any value of identifier is already stored under its type key.

Return type:

bool

keys()[source]¶

Return the type-URI keys of the identifier dict.

values()[source]¶

Return the URI value lists for each type key.

items()[source]¶

Return (type_key, uri_list) pairs for all identifier entries.

iter_pairs()[source]¶

Yield (type_key, uri) pairs for every individual URI value across all types.

Return type:

Iterator[tuple]

classmethod from_json(data, _context=None)[source]¶

Deserialize an IdentifierList from a dict mapping type-URIs to lists of value strings.

Return type:

IdentifierList

property value: dict | None¶

Return {"resource": uri} if a URI is set, or None.

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True, 'extra': 'allow', 'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(context, /)¶

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Return type:

None

Args:

self: The BaseModel instance. context: The context.

Serialize, deserialize, and resolve references across GedcomX object graphs.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.serialization.ResolveStats(total_refs=0, cache_hits=0, cache_misses=0, resolved_ok=0, resolved_fail=0, by_ref_type=<factory>, by_target_type=<factory>, failures=<factory>, attempts=<factory>, resolver_time_ms=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Telemetry counters and details collected during a reference-resolution pass.

total_refs: int = 0¶
cache_hits: int = 0¶
cache_misses: int = 0¶
resolved_ok: int = 0¶
resolved_fail: int = 0¶
by_ref_type: Dict[str, int]¶
by_target_type: Dict[str, int]¶
failures: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
attempts: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
resolver_time_ms: float = 0.0¶
note_attempt(*, ref_type, key, path, cache_hit)[source]¶

Record a resolution attempt, incrementing total and cache hit/miss counters.

Return type:

None

note_success(*, target)[source]¶

Record a successful resolution and increment the target-type counter.

Return type:

None

note_failure(*, ref_type, key, path, reason)[source]¶

Record a failed resolution with the failure reason.

Return type:

None

note_resolver_time(dt_ms)[source]¶

Accumulate resolver wall-clock time in milliseconds.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.serialization.Serialization[source]¶

Bases: object

Static utilities for serializing GedcomX objects to JSON-compatible dicts and deserializing them back.

static serialize(obj)[source]¶

Serialize a GedcomX object (or primitive) to a JSON-compatible dict/value.

Returns:

A JSON-serializable representation, or None if the input is None or empty.

classmethod apply_resource_resolutions(inst, resolver)[source]¶

Resolve any queued attribute setters stored on the instance.

Return type:

None

classmethod deserialize(data, class_type, *, resolver=None, queue_setters=True)[source]¶

Deserialize a JSON dict into an instance of class_type.

data should contain keys that correspond to class_type fields. If resolver is provided, Resource and URI references are resolved immediately; otherwise unresolved references may be queued as lazy setters when queue_setters is true.

Return type:

Any

classmethod resolve_references_recursive(root, resolver)[source]¶

Walk the graph rooted at root and resolve all gedcomx.Resource instances in-place (or by replacing container elements). - Handles dict/list/tuple/set - Uses SCHEMA to traverse fields on your model objects - Applies any queued _resource_setters - Avoids cycles and reuses a small cache so the same Resource isn’t resolved multiple times. Returns the (possibly same) root.

Return type:

Any

Conversion routines between GEDCOM 5.x, GEDCOM 7, and GedcomX models.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conversion.GxoObjectStack[source]¶

Bases: object

Level-indexed object stack (GEDCOM ‘level’ -> current GX object at that level)

This replaces the old push/pop hybrid behavior with what your converter actually uses everywhere: self.object_map[level] = obj and self.object_map[level-1].

get(level, default=None)[source]¶

Return the object at the given level, or default if not present.

Return type:

Any

items()[source]¶

Return (level, object) pairs for all entries in the stack.

clear()[source]¶

Remove all entries from the stack.

Return type:

None

parent(record)[source]¶

Return the object at the level directly above the given record.

Return type:

Any

ancestor(record, up)[source]¶

Return the object up levels above the given record.

Return type:

Any

set_level(level, value)[source]¶

Set the object at a given level and return it.

Return type:

Any

items_desc()[source]¶

Return (level, object) pairs sorted from highest level to lowest.

Return type:

List[tuple[int, Any]]

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.conversion.GedcomConverter[source]¶

Bases: object

Converts a parsed Gedcom5x object graph into a GedcomX genealogy.

Uses a tag-dispatch table (handle_<TAG> methods) to walk every GEDCOM element and produce the equivalent GedcomX objects. Call Gedcom5x_GedcomX() to run the full conversion.

type_name_type = {'aka': NameType.AlsoKnownAs}¶
personal_events = {'BARM', 'BASM', 'BLES', 'CENS', 'CHRA', 'CONF', 'CREM', 'EMIG', 'GRAD', 'NATU', 'ORDN', 'RETI', 'WILL'}¶
property ignored_tags¶

Return a dict of unhandled tag counts, or None if all tags were handled.

static clean_str(text)[source]¶

Strip whitespace and remove HTML tags from a string; returns ‘’ for None.

Return type:

str

parse_gedcom5x_record(record)[source]¶

Dispatch a single GEDCOM Element to its registered handler and recurse into subrecords.

Return type:

None

Args:

record: The GEDCOM Element to process.

Raises:

AssertionError: If record is None. ConversionErrorDump: On unhandled exceptions during conversion.

convert_exception_dump(record, note=None)[source]¶

Library-safe dump: logs full stack context, then raises ConversionErrorDump. No prints.

Return type:

None

static format_counts_table(counts)[source]¶

Format a counts mapping as a multi-column text table sorted by count descending.

Return type:

str

static has_duplicates(seq)[source]¶

Return True if the sequence contains any duplicate values.

Return type:

bool

static find_duplicates(seq)[source]¶

Return a list of values that appear more than once in seq, preserving order.

unique(seq, key=None)[source]¶

Return a deduplicated list preserving first-occurrence order.

Return type:

List[TypeVar(T)]

Args:

seq: Input iterable. key: Optional callable to extract a comparison key from each element.

Gedcom5x_GedcomX(gedcom5x)[source]¶

Convert a parsed Gedcom5x object to a GedcomX genealogy.

Primes id maps from GEDCOM cross-references, then walks every top-level record through the tag dispatch table to populate the GedcomX object.

Return type:

GedcomX

Args:

gedcom5x: A fully parsed Gedcom5x instance.

Returns:

The populated GedcomX genealogy.

Raises:

ValueError: If gedcom5x is falsy.

handle__apid(record)[source]¶

Handle _APID tag: store an Ancestry PID as an identifier on the parent SourceReference or SourceDescription.

handle__meta(record)[source]¶

Handle _META tag: add its value as a Note to the parent SourceDescription or DocumentParsingContainer.

handle__wlnk(record)[source]¶

Handle _WLNK tag: delegate to the SOUR handler.

handle_adop(record)[source]¶

Handle ADOP tag: add an Adoption fact to the parent Person.

handle_addr(record)[source]¶

Handle ADDR tag: add an Address to the parent Agent.

handle_adr1(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR1 tag: set street on the parent Address.

handle_adr2(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR2 tag: set street2 on the parent Address.

handle_adr3(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR3 tag: set street3 on the parent Address.

handle_adr4(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR4 tag: set street4 on the parent Address.

handle_adr5(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR5 tag: set street5 on the parent Address.

handle_adr6(record)[source]¶

Handle ADR6 tag: set street6 on the parent Address.

handle_abbr(record)[source]¶

Handle ABBR tag: add an abbreviation note to the parent SourceDescription.

handle_agnc(record)[source]¶

Handle AGNC tag: add an agency note to the parent Fact or SourceDescription.

handle_auth(record)[source]¶

Handle AUTH tag: find or create an Agent for the author and attach to the parent SourceDescription.

handle_phon(record)[source]¶

Handle PHON tag: append a phone URI to the parent Agent.

handle_email(record)[source]¶

Handle EMAIL tag: append an email URI to the parent Agent.

handle_fax(record)[source]¶

Handle FAX tag: append a FAX:-prefixed URI to the parent Agent’s emails list.

handle_bapm(record)[source]¶

Handle BAPM tag: add a Baptism fact to the parent Person.

handle_birt(record)[source]¶

Handle BIRT tag: add a Birth fact to the parent Person.

handle_buri(record)[source]¶

Handle BURI tag: add a Burial fact to the parent Person.

handle_caln(record)[source]¶

Handle CALN tag: add a call-number identifier to the parent SourceReference or SourceDescription.

handle_chan(record)[source]¶

Handle CHAN tag: set a change date or Attribution on the parent SourceDescription, Agent, or Person.

handle_chr(record)[source]¶

Handle CHR tag: add a Christening fact to the parent Person.

handle_city(record)[source]¶

Handle CITY tag: set the city field on the parent Address.

handle_conc(record)[source]¶

Handle CONC tag: concatenate the value onto the parent object’s text or name field.

handle_cont(record)[source]¶

Handle CONT tag: append a continuation line to the parent object’s text or value field.

handle_crea(record)[source]¶

Handle CREA tag: set the creation date on the parent SourceDescription or Agent Attribution.

handle__crea(record)[source]¶

Handle _CREA tag: set the created date on the parent SourceDescription from the record value.

handle_ctry(record)[source]¶

Handle CTRY tag: set the country on the parent Address.

handle_data(record)[source]¶

Handle DATA tag: forward the parent object to the same stack level (pass-through).

Return type:

None

handle_date(record)[source]¶

Handle DATE tag: set the date on the parent Fact, Event, SourceDescription, or Attribution.

handle_deat(record)[source]¶

Handle DEAT tag: add a Death fact to the parent Person.

handle_pevent(record)[source]¶

Handle personal event tags (BARM, BASM, etc.) that map to FactType via fact_from_even_tag.

handle_even(record)[source]¶

Handle EVEN tag: create a Fact or Event on the parent Person or SourceDescription based on the event type.

handle_exid(record)[source]¶

Handle EXID tag: add an External identifier to the parent subject.

handle_fam(record)[source]¶

Handle FAM tag: reset the family parser for a new family record.

Return type:

None

handle_husb(record)[source]¶

Handle HUSB tag: resolve the husband Person and register them on the family parser.

handle_wife(record)[source]¶

Handle WIFE tag: resolve the wife Person and register them on the family parser.

handle_chil(record)[source]¶

Handle CHIL tag: resolve the child Person and add them to the family parser.

handle_famc(_record)[source]¶

Handle FAMC tag: child-to-family link (not yet implemented; placeholder).

Return type:

None

handle_fams(_record)[source]¶

Handle FAMS tag: spouse-to-family link (not yet implemented; placeholder).

Return type:

None

handle_file(record)[source]¶

Handle FILE tag: set the resource URL on the parent SourceDescription or DocumentParsingContainer.

handle_form(record)[source]¶

Handle FORM tag: map media format to MIME type on a SourceDescription, or place name on a PlaceDescription.

handle_medi(record)[source]¶

Handle GEDCOM 5 FILE.MEDI (media qualifier: photo, video, audio, …).

No direct GedcomX equivalent; the value is stored as a note on the parent SourceDescription so the information is not lost. MEDI under REPO.CALN has no GX mapping and is silently ignored.

Return type:

None

handle_fsid(record)[source]¶

Handle FSID tag: add a FamilySearch identifier to the parent subject.

handle_givn(record)[source]¶

Handle GIVN tag: add a Given name part to the parent Name.

handle_head(record)[source]¶

Handle HEAD tag: create the top-level Attribution block for the GedcomX document.

handle_indi(record)[source]¶

Handle INDI tag: look up or create a Person for this individual record.

handle_immi(record)[source]¶

Handle IMMI tag: add an Immigration Fact to the parent Person.

handle_map(record)[source]¶

Handle MAP tag: descend into the PlaceDescription’s coordinates block.

handle_marr(record)[source]¶

Handle MARR tag: add a Marriage Fact via the family parser (FAM context) or directly on the parent subject.

When the parent tag is FAM the family parser is reused; otherwise add_fact is called directly on the parent object.

handle_lati(record)[source]¶

Handle LATI tag: set the latitude on the parent PlaceDescription.

handle_long(record)[source]¶

Handle LONG tag: set the longitude on the parent PlaceDescription.

handle__link(record)[source]¶

Handle _LINK tag: add an External identifier URL to the description of the parent SourceReference.

handle__milt(record)[source]¶

Handle _MILT tag: add a MilitaryService Fact to the parent Person.

handle_name(record)[source]¶

Handle NAME tag: add a Name to a Person, or a name TextValue to an Agent.

handle_note(record)[source]¶

Handle NOTE tag: add a Note to the appropriate parent object (SourceDescription, Conclusion, Agent, etc.).

handle_nsfx(record)[source]¶

Handle NSFX tag: add a Suffix name part to the parent Name.

handle_occu(record)[source]¶

Handle OCCU tag: add an Occupation Fact to the parent Person.

handle_obje(record)[source]¶

Handle OBJE tag: create or look up a SourceDescription for a multimedia object record.

handle_page(record)[source]¶

Handle PAGE tag: add a Page qualifier to the parent SourceReference.

handle_plac(record)[source]¶

Handle PLAC tag: create or look up a PlaceDescription and attach it to the parent Fact, Event, or SourceDescription.

handle_post(record)[source]¶

Handle POST tag: set the postal code on the parent Address.

handle_publ(record)[source]¶

Handle PUBL tag: set the publisher Agent (or publication date) on the parent SourceDescription.

handle_prob(record)[source]¶

Handle PROB tag: add a Probate Fact to the parent Person.

handle_uid(record)[source]¶

Handle UID tag: add a Primary identifier (UID URI) to the parent subject.

handle_refn(record)[source]¶

Handle REFN tag: add a Reference Number External identifier to the parent Person, SourceDescription, or Agent.

handle_repo(record)[source]¶

Handle REPO tag: create or look up a repository Agent, or link it to a SourceDescription.

handle_resi(record)[source]¶

Handle RESI tag: add a Residence Fact to the parent Person.

handle_rin(record)[source]¶

Handle RIN tag: add a Record Identification Number as an External identifier note on the parent SourceDescription.

handle_sex(record)[source]¶

Handle SEX tag: set the Gender (Male/Female/Unknown) on the parent Person.

handle_sour(record)[source]¶

Handle SOUR tag: create or look up a SourceDescription (level 0) or add a SourceReference to the parent conclusion.

handle_stae(record)[source]¶

Handle STAE tag: set the state or province on the parent Address.

handle_subm(record)[source]¶

Handle SUBM tag: create or look up a submitter Agent and optionally set it as the Attribution contributor.

handle_surn(record)[source]¶

Handle SURN tag: add a Surname name part to the parent Name.

handle_text(record)[source]¶

Handle TEXT tag: add transcribed text as a TextValue on a SourceReference’s description or as a Document on a SourceDescription.

handle_titl(record)[source]¶

Handle TITL tag: add a title TextValue to the parent SourceDescription, or a Title name part to a Name.

handle_tran(record)[source]¶

Handle TRAN (Translation/Transliteration) tags.

GEDCOM 5.5.1 uses TRAN to provide alternate language/script forms of text-bearing records. GedcomX represents these as additional NameForms (for Name records) or additional Notes/TextValues (for text records). The LANG child tag is processed by handle_lang, which sets the lang attribute on whatever this method stores in the object_map.

handle_lang(record)[source]¶

Set the lang attribute on the current object (child of TRAN/FONE).

handle_type(record)[source]¶

Handle TYPE tag: refine the type of the parent Event, Fact, Identifier, or Name.

handle__url(record)[source]¶

Handle _URL tag: set the about URL on the grandparent SourceDescription.

handle_www(record)[source]¶

Handle WWW tag: set the homepage on an Agent, or add a URL identifier to a SourceReference’s description.

Schema registry and type-introspection helpers for GedcomX models.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.Schema[source]¶

Bases: object

Central registry of fields for classes.

  • field_type_table: {“ClassName”: {“field”: <type or type-string>}}

  • URI/Resource preference in unions: URI > Resource > first-declared

  • Optional/None is stripped

  • Containers are preserved; their inner args are normalized recursively

set_uri_class(cls)[source]¶

Register the URI class used by schema helpers.

Return type:

None

set_resource_class(cls)[source]¶

Register the Resource class used by schema helpers.

Return type:

None

register_class(cls, *, mapping=None, include_bases=True, use_annotations=True, use_init=True, overwrite=False, ignore=None, toplevel=False, toplevel_meta=None)[source]¶

Introspect and register fields for a class.

  • reads class __annotations__ (preferred) or __init__ annotations

  • merges base classes (MRO) if include_bases=True

  • applies mapping overrides last

  • normalizes each type:

    strip Optional → prefer URI/Resource → collapse union to single

Return type:

None

register_extra(cls, name, typ, *, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Register a single extra field (normalized) and propagate to subclasses.

For pydantic GedcomXModel subclasses, also calls define_ext() so the field becomes a proper model field (not just an extra-captured value).

Return type:

None

normalize_all()[source]¶

Re-run normalization across all registered fields.

Return type:

None

get_class_fields(type_name)[source]¶

Return normalised field map for type_name.

Explicit registrations (via register_class) take priority. For any GedcomXModel subclass that hasn’t been explicitly registered, the fields are auto-derived from Pydantic model_fields and cached so that the resolver and gxcli type-inference work without needing manual decoration on every model class.

Return type:

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

set_toplevel(cls, *, meta=None)[source]¶

Register a class as a top-level GedcomX type.

Return type:

None

is_toplevel(cls_or_name)[source]¶

Return whether the class is registered as top-level.

Return type:

bool

is_toplevel_obj(obj)[source]¶

Like is_toplevel, but takes an arbitrary object and checks its class.

Return type:

bool

get_toplevel()[source]¶

Return the registered top-level classes.

Return type:

Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]

get_extras(cls_or_name)[source]¶

Direct extras declared on this class (not inherited).

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

get_all_extras(cls_or_name)[source]¶

Direct + inherited extras for this class.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

property json: dict[str, dict[str, str]]¶

Return a JSON-compatible representation of the current data.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.type_repr(tp)[source]¶

Return a readable string representation of a type annotation.

Return type:

str

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_to_jsonable(schema)[source]¶

Convert schema metadata into JSON-serializable structures.

Return type:

dict[str, dict[str, str]]

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_class(mapping=None, *, include_bases=True, use_annotations=True, use_init=True, overwrite=False, ignore=None, toplevel=False, toplevel_meta=None)[source]¶

Decorator to register a class with SCHEMA at import time.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.bind_schema_property(cls, fget, *, schema, name=None, typ=None, fset=None, fdel=None, overwrite=False, doc=None)[source]¶

Bind module-level getter (and optional setter/deleter) as a property on cls, and register it in schema.

Return type:

property

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_property(*, schema, typ=None, name=None, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Use on a method inside a class.

Example:

@schema_property(schema=SCHEMA) def display_name(self) -> str: …

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.schema_property_plugin(*, name=None, typ=None)[source]¶

Create a plugin that binds a computed schema property.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.apply_schema_property(cls, fget, *, schema, overwrite=False)[source]¶

Apply a schema-bound computed property to a class.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.accept_extras(*, stash_attr='_extras', allow_only=None)[source]¶

Wraps the class __init__ to swallow unknown kwargs, and propagates to subclasses. - If a subclass defines its own __init__, it gets wrapped automatically via __init_subclass__. - Unknown kwargs are attached as attributes, or stashed in stash_attr if __slots__ present. - If allow_only is provided (callable(cls) -> set[str]), only those keys are accepted.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.extensible(*, allow_only=None, **schema_kwargs)[source]¶

Apply schema_class then accept_extras in the right order. Example: @extensible(toplevel=True)

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.extensible_dataclass(*d_args, allow_only=None, **schema_kwargs)[source]¶

Decorate a dataclass so it participates in the extension system.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.ExtrasAwareMeta[source]¶

Bases: type

Metaclass that intercepts cls(*args, **kwargs) and ensures registered extras are accepted.

  • Only passes constructor-known kwargs into __init__.

  • Allowed extras (from SCHEMA) are attached after construction.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.fact_from_even_tag(even_value)[source]¶

Return the GedcomX fact type mapped from a GEDCOM EVEN tag.

gedcomtools.gedcomx.schemas.event_from_even_tag(even_value)[source]¶

Return the GedcomX event type mapped from a GEDCOM EVEN tag.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.Extensible(**data)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXModel

Base class for GedcomX entities that support dynamic field extensions.

Inherits define_ext() / declared_extras() from GedcomXModel.

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True, 'extra': 'allow', 'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.TrustLevel(*values)[source]¶

Bases: IntEnum

Coarse gate controlling which plugin sources may be loaded.

Levels are ordered: DISABLED < BUILTIN < LOCAL < ALL.

DISABLED = 0¶
BUILTIN = 1¶
LOCAL = 2¶
ALL = 3¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.PluginStatus(*values)[source]¶

Bases: str, Enum

Capture the load status and metadata for a plugin entry.

PENDING = 'pending'¶
ALLOWED = 'allowed'¶
LOADED = 'loaded'¶
FAILED = 'failed'¶
BLOCKED = 'blocked'¶
class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.PluginEntry(name, source, status=PluginStatus.PENDING, expected_sha256=None, actual_sha256=None, error=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Describe an allowed plugin source and its trust metadata.

name: str¶
source: str¶
status: PluginStatus = 'pending'¶
expected_sha256: str | None = None¶
actual_sha256: str | None = None¶
error: Exception | None = None¶
exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.PluginBlockedError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when a plugin is blocked by the current trust level.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.RegistryLockedError[source]¶

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when the registry is modified after load() has been called.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.PluginRegistry[source]¶

Bases: object

Secure, allowlist-based plugin registry.

Usage:

from gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible import plugin_registry, TrustLevel

plugin_registry.set_trust_level(TrustLevel.LOCAL)
plugin_registry.allow("gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensions.fs")
plugin_registry.allow("./plugins/my_ext.py")
plugin_registry.allow("https://example.com/ext.zip", sha256="abc123…")
result = plugin_registry.load()

Configuration must happen before load(). After load() the registry is locked — calling allow() or set_trust_level() raises RegistryLockedError.

set_trust_level(level)[source]¶

Set the coarse trust gate. Must be called before load().

Return type:

None

property trust_level: TrustLevel¶

Return the configured plugin trust level.

property is_locked: bool¶

Return whether the plugin registry has been locked by loading.

allow(source, *, name=None, sha256=None)[source]¶

Explicitly allow a plugin to be loaded.

source may be a module name, local file or directory path, or an HTTPS URL. sha256 is required for URL sources and must match the downloaded content. Raises RegistryLockedError if called after load() and ValueError if a URL source omits sha256.

Return type:

None

load(base_package='gedcomx', *, recursive=False, root_package='gedcomtools')[source]¶

Import all explicitly-allowed plugins. May only be called once.

Returns a dict with "imported" and "errors" keys. Raises RegistryLockedError if called more than once.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

load_one(name, base_package='gedcomx', *, recursive=False, root_package='gedcomtools')[source]¶

Import a single explicitly-allowed plugin by name.

Unlike load(), this does not lock the registry, so it may be called multiple times for different entries.

Returns a dict with "imported" and "errors" keys. Raises KeyError if no entry with the requested name exists.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

list()[source]¶

Return a snapshot of all registered plugin entries.

Return type:

List[PluginEntry]

gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.set_trust_level(level)[source]¶

Convenience wrapper: set trust level on the global plugin_registry.

Return type:

None

gedcomtools.gedcomx.extensible.import_plugins(base_package, *, subpackage='extensions', local_dir='./plugins', env_var='GEDCOMX_PLUGINS', recursive=False, root_package='gedcomtools', registry=None)[source]¶

Scan-based plugin loader gated by trust level.

Returns {"imported": [...], "errors": {...}}. The registry defaults to the global plugin_registry; pass a fresh PluginRegistry() in tests to avoid global state.

Trust level behavior: DISABLED returns immediately, BUILTIN loads the bundled subpackage only, LOCAL also loads local paths, and ALL permits URL sources as well.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.mutations.GedcomXObject(record)[source]¶

Bases: object

Base wrapper capturing the GEDCOM5 record that spawned a GedcomX object.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.mutations.GedcomXSourceOrDocument(record)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXObject

Accumulates metadata fields for a GEDCOM SOUR or OBJE record before creating a SourceDescription.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.mutations.GedcomXEventOrFact(record)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXObject

Factory that returns the correct Fact or Event instance for a GEDCOM5 tag.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.mutations.GedcomXRelationshipBuilder(record)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXObject

Placeholder builder for constructing complex GEDCOM relationship structures.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.family.FamilyParser(gx)[source]¶

Bases: object

Stateful parser that accumulates FAM record fields and commits couple and parent-child relationships to a GedcomX object.

reset()[source]¶

Finalize the current family then reset for the next FAM record.

add_source_reference(source_ref)[source]¶

Add a SourceReference to the marriage fact.

add_note(note)[source]¶

Add a Note to the marriage fact.

set_marr_date(record)[source]¶

Set the marriage date from a GEDCOM DATE element.

set_marr_plac(record)[source]¶

Set the marriage place from a GEDCOM PLAC element, creating a PlaceDescription if needed.

set_husband(husband)[source]¶

Assign the husband (person1) of the couple relationship.

set_wife(wife)[source]¶

Assign the wife (person2) of the couple relationship.

finalize()[source]¶

Commit the couple relationship to the GedcomX graph.

Only called when the FAM record is fully parsed. A couple relationship requires both persons; if only one is present the family had no partner recorded and we skip the couple (parent-child relationships were already created directly in add_child). Marriage facts are attached to persons only when the couple is complete.

Return type:

None

add_child(child)[source]¶

Create ParentChild relationships between the child and each known parent.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomHeaderField(key, value)[source]¶

Bases: object

A key/value pair used in MANIFEST.MF entries.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomResource(path, headers=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Represents a single resource entry in a GedcomX ZIP manifest.

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomManifest[source]¶

Bases: object

Builds and renders the META-INF/MANIFEST.MF content for a GedcomX ZIP package.

add(path, content_type='application/x-gedcomx-v1+json')[source]¶

Register a resource entry at path with the given content-type header.

Return type:

None

render()[source]¶

Render as a MANIFEST.MF-style text block.

Return type:

str

class gedcomtools.gedcomx.zip.GedcomZip(path=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Read and write GedcomX ZIP file packages with a MANIFEST.MF and JSON resource entries.

add_object_as_resource(obj)[source]¶

Serialize obj and store it as a JSON entry inside the zip.

  • If obj is a GedcomX instance it is written as genealogy.json and the method returns immediately — no second serialization pass.

  • For any other registered top-level type, the entry is named after the object’s id or class name.

  • Returns the internal archive name on success, or None if obj is not a recognised top-level type.

Return type:

str | None

write_manifest()[source]¶

Write META-INF/MANIFEST.MF based on all added resources.

Return type:

None

close()[source]¶

Write the manifest and close the zip. Safe to call multiple times.

Return type:

None

classmethod read(path)[source]¶

Read a GedcomX ZIP archive and return a merged GedcomX instance.

Looks for all .json entries (excluding the manifest), deserializes each one, and merges them into a single GedcomX object. The primary entry genealogy.json is processed first.

Return type:

GedcomX

Raises:

FileNotFoundError: If path does not exist. ValueError: If no GedcomX JSON entries are found in the archive.

classmethod list_entries(path)[source]¶

Return a list of entry info dicts from a GedcomX ZIP archive.

Each dict has name, size, compress_size. Manifest entries are included.

Return type:

list[dict]

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.GedcomXError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base for all app-specific errors.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.GedcomClassAttributeError(*args)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXError

Document the GedcomClassAttributeError type.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.TagConversionError(record, levelstack)[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXError

Document the TagConversionError type.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.ConversionErrorDump[source]¶

Bases: GedcomXError

Document the ConversionErrorDump type.

exception gedcomtools.gedcomx.exceptions.GedcomXDateParseError[source]¶

Bases: ValueError

Raised when we cannot parse a date string into GEDCOM X Date format.
